signals.reasoning: This live trade remains broken. BLUR has gone from a peak gain of about $1.21 to a current loss near $-0.48, retaining none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. There is no management case for staying in it.
BTC is stable and liquid, but the board is still no-trade and the live veto argues against opening fresh risk. Neutral is the right posture.
ETH has slightly constructive price action, but funding is positive and the board remains no-trade. Not enough edge to matter.
SOL is modestly constructive but still low-edge, with positive funding and no-trade board status. Easy hold.
ZEC is one of the strongest names on the board with real liquidity, strong momentum, and negative funding, but it is already extended and the active veto keeps it in watch-only mode.
DASH has good momentum and volume, but the setup is still neutral and positive funding keeps it from becoming a clean squeeze candidate.
MERL is moving, but liquidity is thin and the board is still neutral. Not compelling enough to act on.
ZEN has decent strength, but funding is positive and the board remains neutral. Watchlist only under current conditions.
GOAT is the cleaner fade among the non-position names because it is crowded, expensive on funding, and still thin enough to be vulnerable. I would not buy this setup.
PEOPLE is too thin and too neutral to matter. No strong edge either way.
TNSR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the active veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist name, not a live trade.
